Radix Occulta
====
The Hidden Root
==== {{Underline|555}}
Aurelia Suffocans
=
Object 52
= {{Underline|555}} {{RecommendedAudioNest|font='Habibi', Serif|bg-color=var(--theme-page-background-color--secondary)|border=1px solid var(--theme-border-color)|text-color=var(--theme-text-color)|audio={{Audio|file=Ytmp3free.cc drifting-time-misplaced-the-caretaker-stage-3-youtubemp3free.org.mp3|align=center|round=yes|volume=0.3|secrettext=I'm in your walls}}}}
[[File:Aurelia Suffocans.png|center|300px]]
== == A vine of hearts climbs up the stone,
To claim the soil for its own.
One drop of pleasure to clear one's mind,
But leave a burning fire behind.
Its cooling sap becomes an unbreakable chain,
As emerald ink bleeds through one's vein.
==
Description
== +++'''Object 52''', also known as '''''“Aurelia Suffocans,”''''' '''''“Auresuffo,”''''' and the '''''“Chill Weeds,”''''' is a highly invasiveA species that adversely affect habitats and bioregions, which cause ecological, and environmental damage. twining vine with a decorative appearance. Its foliage grow with heart-shaped leaves characterized by a distinctive metallic, and brassy sheen caused by a reflective cuticular layerA cuticle layer is a protecting film which covers the outermost skin layer of leaves on the leaf epidermis.The epidermis is a thin, outermost, and avascular layer of skin that acts as a vital protective barrier against natural hazards The plant grows groups of small, and pale yellow flowers. They emit a acrid odor of burnt sugar mixed with ozone. +++The species appears to have fast vegetative growth, particularly in humid environments, where its height could grow up to 30 centimeters a day. Its flexible stems have strong thigmotropic behaviorThigmotropism is the directional growth movement of vines and allows them to wrap around nearby both natural and manmade structures. The plant can grow in a somewhat short time compared to other plant species, covering vast areas with its vines; thus its invasive plant classification. +++'''Aurelia Suffocans''' reproduces through not only sexual but also vegetative propagation. The plant releases microscopic, and sharp structures. These barbed propagules function similarly to burrs, which stick onto [[Entity List|entities fur]] or fabric, which aid distant dispersal. Additionally, its seeds have a heat-resistant outer cover, which protects them in the soil for up to ten years before germinatingGermination is the process by which an organism grows from a seed or spore under suitable conditions. +++'''Auresuffo''' has strong vegetative regeneration. Any chopped stem contains active meristematic tissueMeristematic tissue consists of meristematic cells that have the capacity to divide and form new cells that can produce new roots upon contact with soil or moisture. Due to this, attempting to remove it only makes its spread faster instead of stopping it. ==
Usage
== +++Despite its beauty, '''Aurelia Suffocans''' has been avoided because of a widely unlawful use due to the psychoactive compounds present in the sap found within its thickest stem nodes.Plant stem supports leaves, flowers and fruits, transports water and dissolved substances between the roots and the shoots in the xylem and phloem, engages in photosynthesis, stores nutrients, and produces new living tissue When harvested, this sap can be boiled and reduced into a dark, sticky, and glass-like resin named by harvesters as '''''“Aurelia-Black,”''''' usually shortened to '''''“Aureliack.”''''' +++The hardened substance is inhaled as vapor, or eaten in small parts. Its popularity originates from a tense neurological stimulation caused shortly after consumption. +++The initial effect lasts approximately for thirty minutes. During this, it gives better eyesight, and sharpened cognitive focus, although color perception and thoughts become unnatural and absurd. The body experiences significant temporary pain signals reduction, as well. Many individuals report feeling emotionally calm during this stage. These effects make '''Aureliack''' particularly attractive in stressful situations. +++A significant physiological effect of the compound is caused by an alkaloidAlkaloids are a broad class of naturally occurring organic compounds containing at least one nitrogen atom. They are known for having pronounced physiological effects on humans, which include medicinal uses and stimulant properties found within the plant’s sap. This chemical firstly imitates a body’s natural temperature by making a pleasant sensation of cooling throughout the body, albeit this fake cooling effect later negatively interrupts the body’s normal temperature regulation after the drug metabolizes. This will cause the individual to feel a tense feeling of internal heat, described as “burning from the inside out.” This occurs even when external temperature is normal. Individuals will consume more to stop the pain, which leads to dependency. +++Prolonged usage of the alkaloids leads vascular tissues to gradually stiffen and discolor, which produces a visible pale green tint along the veins beneath the skin. Although it is not suddenly fatal, this reaction is a sign of severe chemical saturation within the bloodstream. +++Avoiding '''Aureliack''' after usage causes severe neurological and physiological discomfort. Individuals frequently experience extreme paranoia, anxiety, and perceptional distortion, with a sudden and complete loss of voice. This effect is believed to be the result of residual resin clotting and irritating the throat during consumption or inhalation of the resin. ==
Location
== +++'''Aurelia Suffocans''' can be found growing around trees, poles, and fences, as well as spreading across brick walls, barriers, and other structures in areas where high humidity is present. It is commonly documented in [[Level 2]], [[Level 6]], [[Level 9]], [[Level 13]], etc. ==
Discovery
== +++Before its identification, '''Aurelia Suffocans''' was repeatedly misclassified as a relative of the common sweet potato plant due to its similar vine structure and heart-shaped leaves. Early inspections by [[Another Dimension Foundation]] noticed its unusually fast growth, but these traits were mistaken as environmental variation, as well. +++Further study revealed several inconsistencies, including: its metallic leaf sheen, strong regeneration ability, and fast spreading. These characteristics led [[Another Dimension Foundation|A.D.F.]] researchers to re-examine samples; finally confirming that it is a new and undocumented plant. Formal documentation, this documentation you are currently reading, was written shortly after its discovery. == == {{Footnotes}} == == {{Blurbackground|file=Old paper bg.jpg}} {{Collapse|body=
